name: Nick LaRocca
abstract: Dominic James "Nick" LaRocca (April 11, 1889 – February 22, 1961), was an American early jazz cornetist and trumpeter and the leader of the Original Dixieland Jass Band, who is credited by some as being "the father of modern jazz". He is the composer of one of the most recorded jazz classics of all-time, "Tiger Rag".

about_quotes.speaker: William T. (Bill) Challis
about_quotes.evidence: I liked Nick LaRocca, the way he played a melody, and I liked the sound of his melody and his cornet.

about_quotes.speaker: Loren M. "Tex" Brewster
about_quotes.evidence: I have played for one or two weeks with the original Dixieland jazz band the uh cornet player or trumpet player had to take a week off and he got me to sit in a week or play for a week or two weeks whatever it was for him

about_quotes.speaker: Armand Hug
about_quotes.evidence: I worked with him right towards the end. When he died, I worked right up to the time that he died. I wrote about, I guess maybe, 15, 20 tunes with him.

about_quotes.speaker: "Papa Jack" Laine
about_quotes.evidence: I had this man, La Rocca, in the band. Nick La Rocca? He played with me, too.

about_quotes.speaker: William Russell
about_quotes.evidence: Actually the original Dixieland was the first to record. It might have been of course they always called it ragtime. A lot has been made like for instance in Brun's book on Nick LaRocca always made a big point of that.

about_quotes.speaker: Harold E. Peterson
about_quotes.evidence: Now, if Nick LaRocca would have had Nick LaRocca and the original Dixieland jazz band, Nick LaRocca would have probably got an awful lot of publicity out of that thing, eh? But he didn't, he didn't use the name...
